title.en: Physical or Logical Separation of Information Flows

prose.en: Separate information flows logically or physically using {param:ac-4.21_prm_1} to accomplish {param:ac-04.21_odp.03}.

guidance.en: Enforcing the separation of information flows associated with defined types of data can enhance protection by ensuring that information is not commingled while in transit and by enabling flow control by transmission paths that are not otherwise achievable. Types of separable information include inbound and outbound communications traffic, service requests and responses, and information of differing security impact or classification levels.

prose.en: information flows are separated logically using {param:ac-04.21_odp.01} to accomplish {param:ac-04.21_odp.03};
information flows are separated physically using {param:ac-04.21_odp.02} to accomplish {param:ac-04.21_odp.03}.

objects.en: system configuration settings and associated documentation
list of required separation of information flows by information types
list of mechanisms and/or techniques used to logically or physically separate information flows
Organizational personnel with information flow enforcement responsibilities
organizational personnel with information security responsibilities
Mechanisms implementing information flow enforcement functions

guidelines.en: mechanisms and/or techniques used to logically separate information flows are defined (if selected);
mechanisms and/or techniques used to physically separate information flows are defined (if selected);
required separations by types of information are defined;

ac-04.21_odp.03.alt-label: required separations by types of information
